tasks.description:
Extract `useEditorPreferences` hook.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEditorPreferences.ts` containing:
- Types: `ClipTab` (line 27), `EditorPreferences` interface (lines 29-37), `defaultPreferences` constant (lines 39-47)
- From `useTimelineData`: the `useEditorSettings` call (line 167), `scale` constant (line 171), `scaleWidth` derivation (line 172)
- Callbacks: `setScaleWidth` (lines 547-552), `setActiveClipTab` (lines 554-558), `setAssetPanelState` (lines 561-569)
- Parameter: `timelineId: string`
- Return: `{ preferences, scaleWidth, scale, setScaleWidth, setActiveClipTab, setAssetPanelState }`

Update `useTimelineData.ts`: import and call `useEditorPreferences(timelineId)`, destructure results. Remove moved code. Keep `ClipTab` and `EditorPreferences` re-exported from `useTimelineData.ts` via `export type { ClipTab, EditorPreferences } from './useEditorPreferences'` so `ClipPanel.tsx:12` and `useTimelineState.ts:20` imports don't break.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted editor preferences into useEditorPreferences(timelineId), including the useEditorSettings binding, scale/scaleWidth derivation, and the three preference setters. useTimelineData now consumes the hook and re-exports ClipTab and EditorPreferences. Verified in the final batch state with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description:
Extract `useTimelineQueries` hook.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineQueries.ts` containing:
- `timelineQuery` (lines 180-185) and `assetRegistryQuery` (lines 187-192) only
- `saveMutation` stays in save hook (its `onError` calls `scheduleSave`)
- Parameters: `provider: VideoEditorProvider`, `timelineId: string`
- Return: `{ timelineQuery, assetRegistryQuery }`
- Import `timelineQueryKey`, `assetRegistryQueryKey` from `useTimeline`, `loadTimelineJsonFromProvider` from `timeline-data`

Update `useTimelineData.ts`: call `useTimelineQueries(provider, timelineId)`, destructure. Remove moved code.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted timelineQuery and assetRegistryQuery into useTimelineQueries and removed those useQuery calls from useTimelineData. saveMutation was intentionally left in useTimelineData. Verified in the final batch state with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description:
Extract `useDerivedTimeline` hook.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDerivedTimeline.ts` containing:
- **Stability refs**: `configSignatureRef` (line 153) and `stableResolvedConfigRef` (line 154) — CRITICAL: these must move with `resolvedConfig` memo to preserve referential stability that `RemotionPreview.tsx:36` depends on
- All `useMemo` blocks:
  - `resolvedConfig` with signature-based stabilization (lines 474-487)
  - `selectedClip` (lines 488-493)
  - `selectedTrack` (lines 495-502) — needs `getTrackById` import
  - `selectedClipHasPredecessor` (lines 504-514)
  - `compositionSize` (lines 516-518) — needs `parseResolution` import
  - `renderMetadata` (lines 520-537) — needs `secondsToFrames`, `getClipDurationInFrames` imports
  - `trackScaleMap` (lines 539-545)
- Parameters: `data: TimelineData | null`, `selectedClipId: string | null`, `selectedTrackId: string | null`
- Return: `{ resolvedConfig, selectedClip, selectedTrack, selectedClipHasPredecessor, compositionSize, renderMetadata, trackScaleMap }`

Update `useTimelineData.ts`: call after save hook, pass `save.data`, `save.selectedClipId`, `save.selectedTrackId`. Remove moved code and refs.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Moved the derived timeline memos into useDerivedTimeline, including configSignatureRef and stableResolvedConfigRef alongside the resolvedConfig memo so the signature-based stabilization remains intact. useTimelineData now consumes the derived outputs from the hook. Verified in the final batch state with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description:
Extract `useRenderState` hook.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useRenderState.ts` containing:
- State: `renderStatus`, `renderLog`, `renderDirty`, `renderProgress`, `renderResultUrl`, `renderResultFilename` (lines 161-166)
- The `useClientRender` wiring (lines 616-635) — accepts `resolvedConfig` and `renderMetadata` as parameters
- The `renderResultUrl` cleanup portion of the effect (lines 468-470 within lines 462-472) — extract as its own `useEffect` with `[renderResultUrl]` dep
- Parameters: `resolvedConfig: ResolvedTimelineConfig | null`, `renderMetadata: CompositionMetadata | null`
- Return: `{ renderStatus, renderLog, renderDirty, renderProgress, renderResultUrl, renderResultFilename, setRenderStatus, setRenderLog, setRenderDirty, setRenderProgress, startRender }`
- Key: `setRenderDirty` is returned so orchestrator can wire it into save hook's `onSaveSuccessRef`

Update `useTimelineData.ts`: call after derived hook, pass `derived.resolvedConfig`, `derived.renderMetadata`. Remove moved code. The `saveTimer` cleanup (lines 463-466) stays in save hook.

Re-export `RenderStatus` from `useTimelineData.ts` if defined in this file.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted render state and useClientRender wiring into useRenderState, split renderResultUrl cleanup into its own effect there, and re-exported RenderStatus from useTimelineData. useTimelineData now delegates render state to the new hook. Verified in the final batch state with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description:
Extract `useAssetOperations` hook.

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetOperations.ts` containing:
- `uploadAsset` (lines 571-577)
- `registerAsset` (lines 579-586)
- `uploadFiles` (lines 588-594)
- `invalidateAssetRegistry` (lines 596-598)
- `reloadFromServer` does NOT go here (settled decision DECISION-001)
- Parameters: `provider`, `timelineId: string`, `userId: string | null`, `queryClient`
- Import `timelineQueryKey`, `assetRegistryQueryKey` from `useTimeline`
- Return: `{ uploadAsset, registerAsset, uploadFiles, invalidateAssetRegistry }`

Update `useTimelineData.ts`: call asset ops hook, destructure. Remove moved code.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted asset upload/register/invalidate helpers into useAssetOperations as a small wrapper with no dependency on save internals. reloadFromServer remains in useTimelineData. Verified in the final batch state with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description:
Extract `useTimelineSave` hook — the largest extraction (~200+ LOC).

Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts` containing:
- **Pure helpers (module-level)**: `shouldAcceptPolledData` (lines 49-60) and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` (lines 113-143). If hook exceeds 200 LOC, move these to `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts` (settled decision DECISION-004).
- **Refs**: `saveTimer` (148), `lastSavedSignature` (149), `editSeqRef` (150), `savedSeqRef` (151), `dataRef` (152), `lastRegistryDataRef` (155), `selectedClipIdRef` (168), `selectedTrackIdRef` (169), `commitDataRef` (387)
- **State**: `data` (157), `selectedClipId` (158), `selectedTrackId` (159), `saveStatus` (160)
- **Layout effects**: ref syncing (lines 174-178), commitDataRef sync (lines 388-390)
- **`saveMutation`** (lines 194-203) — lives here because `onError` calls `scheduleSave`
- **Functions**: `materializeData` (205-215), `saveTimeline` (217-230), `scheduleSave` (232-245), `commitData` (247-285), `applyTimelineEdit` (287-315), `applyResolvedConfigEdit` (317-332), `patchRegistry` (334-385), `reloadFromServer` (600-614)
- **Effects**: poll sync (lines 392-424), registry sync (lines 426-460), saveTimer cleanup (lines 462-466)
- **CRITICAL wiring**: `saveTimeline`'s `onSuccess` (line 227) currently calls `setRenderDirty(true)`. Replace with `onSaveSuccessRef.current?.()` where `onSaveSuccessRef` is a `React.MutableRefObject<(() => void) | null>` parameter (settled decision DECISION-002).
- Parameters: `timelineQuery`, `assetRegistryQuery`, `provider`, `onSaveSuccessRef`
- Return: `{ data, dataRef, selectedClipId, selectedTrackId, saveStatus, setSelectedClipId, setSelectedTrackId, applyTimelineEdit, applyResolvedConfigEdit, patchRegistry, commitData, reloadFromServer, editSeqRef, savedSeqRef, selectedClipIdRef, selectedTrackIdRef, isLoading: timelineQuery.isLoading && !data }`
- Re-export `SaveStatus` type from this file. Also export `shouldAcceptPolledData` (imported by tests or other code).

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted the save/sync lifecycle into `useTimelineSave`, including saveMutation, commit/save pipeline, polling sync, registry sync, save-timer cleanup, and `reloadFromServer`. Moved `shouldAcceptPolledData` and `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` into `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts` per DECISION-004, wired save success through `onSaveSuccessRef.current?.()`, and re-exported `SaveStatus` plus `shouldAcceptPolledData`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`; `useTimelineSave.ts` is now 183 LOC.

Normalized `useTimelineData.ts` to the thin orchestrator form: it now composes `useEditorPreferences`, `useTimelineQueries`, `useTimelineSave`, `useDerivedTimeline`, `useRenderState`, and `useAssetOperations` in the approved order, with `onSaveSuccessRef.current = () => render.setRenderDirty(true)` set after both hooks exist. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`, confirmed the `UseTimelineDataResult` interface block is unchanged versus `HEAD`, confirmed downstream import sites still point at `useTimelineData`, and measured the file at 126 LOC.

tasks.description:
Final validation — no behavioral changes.

1. `npx tsc --noEmit` — full type check, zero errors
2. Count LOC: orchestrator <150, each sub-hook <200 (allow timeline-save-utils.ts if needed)
3. Verify `UseTimelineDataResult` interface is identical to original (diff the interface block)
4. Verify all imports resolve — grep for `from.*useTimelineData` and confirm no broken imports
5. Run dev server (`npm run dev`) and manually test video editor:
   - Loads timeline data
   - Edit a clip → auto-saves (saveStatus transitions: saved → dirty → saving → saved)
   - Remotion preview does NOT remount/flash on edit (referential stability of resolvedConfig)
   - After auto-save completes, render button shows dirty/stale (render-dirty wiring)
   - Reload from server resets save status to 'saved'
   - Render works end-to-end
6. `git diff` review: confirm only moved code, no logic changes
tasks.executor_notes: Validated the static parts of the final extraction successfully: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors; LOC checks show `useTimelineData.ts` at 126 LOC and every extracted hook under 200 LOC (`useTimelineSave.ts` at 183 LOC with `timeline-save-utils.ts` as the allowed helper file); the `UseTimelineDataResult` interface block matches `HEAD` exactly; and downstream imports of `useTimelineData` still resolve. Diff/status review across the touched hook files is consistent with a pure extraction (one modified orchestrator plus seven new focused hook/util files). Manual video-editor runtime checks could not be completed in this environment because the sandbox blocked both `npm run dev` bind attempts with `listen EPERM`.

watch_items: REFERENTIAL STABILITY: configSignatureRef + stableResolvedConfigRef MUST move into useDerivedTimeline alongside the resolvedConfig memo. If separated, RemotionPreview will remount on every poll cycle.

RENDER-DIRTY WIRING: saveTimeline's onSuccess calls setRenderDirty(true). After split, this becomes onSaveSuccessRef.current?.(). The orchestrator must wire onSaveSuccessRef.current = () => render.setRenderDirty(true) AFTER both save and render hooks are constructed.

HOOK CALL ORDER: prefs → queries → save → derived → render → assets. save needs queries; derived needs save.data; render needs derived.resolvedConfig + derived.renderMetadata. The onSaveSuccessRef is a ref so save can be called before render exists.
reloadFromServer MUST stay in useTimelineSave — it resets editSeqRef/savedSeqRef, calls commitData, sets saveStatus. NOT an asset operation.
saveMutation MUST stay in useTimelineSave — its onError calls scheduleSave, which is a save-internal function.
saveTimer cleanup (lines 462-466) stays in useTimelineSave. renderResultUrl cleanup (lines 468-470) moves to useRenderState as a separate useEffect.

LOC BUDGET: useTimelineSave will likely exceed 200 LOC. Proactively extract shouldAcceptPolledData + buildDataFromCurrentRegistry to lib/timeline-save-utils.ts (~40 LOC savings). This is an acceptable pure-function utility file per DECISION-004.
RE-EXPORTS: useTimelineData.ts must re-export ClipTab, EditorPreferences, SaveStatus, RenderStatus, shouldAcceptPolledData, UseTimelineDataResult so existing downstream imports don't break.
useTimelineState.types.ts:4 uses `typeof useTimelineData` for ReturnType — the orchestrator's return type must remain UseTimelineDataResult (unchanged interface).
The effect at lines 462-472 combines saveTimer cleanup AND renderResultUrl cleanup. These must be split into two separate effects when extracting to different hooks.

sense_checks.question: Does useEditorPreferences correctly accept timelineId and wire through useEditorSettings with the storage key pattern `video-editor:preferences:${timelineId}`? Are ClipTab and EditorPreferences re-exported from useTimelineData.ts?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. useEditorPreferences accepts timelineId, uses the `video-editor:preferences:${timelineId}` storage key via useEditorSettings, and useTimelineData.ts re-exports ClipTab and EditorPreferences.

sense_checks.question: Does useTimelineQueries contain ONLY the two useQuery calls (timelineQuery, assetRegistryQuery)? saveMutation must NOT be here — it references scheduleSave.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. useTimelineQueries contains only the timelineQuery and assetRegistryQuery useQuery calls; saveMutation was not moved into it.

sense_checks.question: Do configSignatureRef and stableResolvedConfigRef live inside useDerivedTimeline alongside the resolvedConfig memo? The signature-based stabilization logic (lines 474-487) must be intact and identical.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. configSignatureRef and stableResolvedConfigRef now live inside useDerivedTimeline next to the resolvedConfig memo, and the signature-based stabilization logic was preserved.

sense_checks.question: Does useRenderState have its own useEffect for renderResultUrl cleanup (URL.revokeObjectURL), separate from the saveTimer cleanup? Does it receive resolvedConfig and renderMetadata as parameters and pass them to useClientRender?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. useRenderState owns a dedicated renderResultUrl cleanup effect and receives resolvedConfig plus renderMetadata for the useClientRender wiring.

sense_checks.question: Is useAssetOperations a thin wrapper (~40 LOC) with NO dependency on save internals? reloadFromServer must NOT be here.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. useAssetOperations is a thin asset helper hook with no save-internal coupling, and reloadFromServer was intentionally left out.

sense_checks.question: Does useTimelineSave accept onSaveSuccessRef and call onSaveSuccessRef.current?.() in saveTimeline's onSuccess instead of setRenderDirty(true) directly? Does it include reloadFromServer? Is shouldAcceptPolledData exported?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `useTimelineSave` accepts `onSaveSuccessRef`, calls `onSaveSuccessRef.current?.()` from `saveTimeline` success handling instead of touching render state directly, keeps `reloadFromServer` inside the hook, and exports `shouldAcceptPolledData` for downstream re-export/use.

sense_checks.question: Is the orchestrator under 150 LOC? Does it wire onSaveSuccessRef.current = () => render.setRenderDirty(true) after both hooks? Is UseTimelineDataResult interface byte-identical to the original? Do all 8+ downstream import sites resolve?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `useTimelineData.ts` is 126 LOC, wires `onSaveSuccessRef.current = () => render.setRenderDirty(true)` after constructing `save` and `render`, the `UseTimelineDataResult` interface block is unchanged versus `HEAD`, and downstream import sites still resolve under `npx tsc --noEmit`.

sense_checks.question: Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass with zero errors? Does manual testing confirm: (a) no Remotion preview remount on edit, (b) render dirty after save, (c) reload resets to 'saved'? Does git diff show only moved code with no logic changes?
sense_checks.executor_note: Partially confirmed. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors, the extracted-hook LOC budget is satisfied, and diff/status review is consistent with extraction-only changes. Manual confirmation of preview stability, render-dirty behavior after save, reload-to-saved, and end-to-end render could not be completed because the sandbox blocked both dev-server startup attempts with `listen EPERM`.

meta_commentary:
**Execution strategy:** T1-T5 are independent extractions that can be done in parallel or any order. Each one removes code from useTimelineData.ts and adds a new hook file. After each, run `npx tsc --noEmit` as a quick gate. T6 (useTimelineSave) depends on T2 because it needs the query return types, but can otherwise proceed independently. T7 (orchestrator rewrite) is the integration step — only attempt after all extractions compile. T8 is manual validation.

**Recommended execution order:** Start with T1 (simplest, ~60 LOC) to validate the extraction pattern. Then T2 and T5 (both small). Then T3 and T4. Then T6 (largest). Then T7 and T8.

**Key gotcha — the combined cleanup effect (line 462-472):** The current code has ONE useEffect that cleans up both `saveTimer` and `renderResultUrl`. When splitting, create two separate effects: saveTimer cleanup in useTimelineSave, renderResultUrl cleanup in useRenderState. Both are cleanup-only effects so this is safe.

**Key gotcha — the `isLoading` derivation:** `isLoading` (line 656) is `timelineQuery.isLoading && !data`. After split, `timelineQuery` is in queries hook and `data` is in save hook. The orchestrator must compute this: `isLoading: queries.timelineQuery.isLoading && !save.data`, OR the save hook can return it directly since it has access to both via its parameters.

**LOC management for T6:** Count lines as you go. The two pure helpers (`shouldAcceptPolledData` ~12 LOC, `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` ~31 LOC) are the escape valve. If the hook body exceeds 200 LOC, move them to `lib/timeline-save-utils.ts` and import. This is explicitly allowed by DECISION-004.

**Import preservation:** After T7, grep `from.*useTimelineData` across the entire project and verify every import resolves. The critical ones: `UseTimelineDataResult` (5 hooks), `ClipTab` (ClipPanel.tsx), `SaveStatus`/`RenderStatus`/`EditorPreferences` (useTimelineState.ts re-exports), `shouldAcceptPolledData` (check if imported anywhere), `typeof useTimelineData` (useTimelineState.types.ts).
